tEn: $π_0$: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control
tZh: $π_0$: 面向通用机器人控制的视觉-语言-动作流模型
sEn: A vision-language-action flow model enables generalist robot policies for complex dexterous tasks.
sZh: 一种视觉-语言-动作流模型实现了面向复杂灵巧任务的通用机器人策略。
absEn: Robot learning holds tremendous promise to unlock the full potential of flexible, general, and dexterous robot systems, as well as to address some of the deepest questions in artificial intelligence. However, bringing robot learning to the level of generality required for effective real-world systems faces major obstacles in terms of data, generalization, and robustness. In this paper, we discuss how generalist robot policies (i.e., robot foundation models) can address these challenges, and how we can design effective generalist robot policies for complex and highly dexterous tasks. We propose a novel flow matching architecture built on top of a pre-trained vision-language model (VLM) to inherit Internet-scale semantic knowledge. We then discuss how this model can be trained on a large and diverse dataset from multiple dexterous robot platforms, including single-arm robots, dual-arm robots, and mobile manipulators. We evaluate our model in terms of its ability to perform tasks in zero shot after pre-training, follow language instructions from people and from a high-level VLM policy, and its ability to acquire new skills via fine-tuning. Our results cover a wide variety of tasks, such as laundry folding, table cleaning, and assembling boxes.
absZh: 机器人学习有望释放灵活、通用、灵巧机器人系统的全部潜力，并解决人工智能领域最深层次的问题。然而，要使机器人学习达到实际系统所需的通用性水平，在数据、泛化能力和鲁棒性方面面临重大障碍。本文探讨了通用机器人策略（即机器人基础模型）如何应对这些挑战，以及如何为复杂且高度灵巧的任务设计有效的通用机器人策略。我们提出了一种基于预训练视觉-语言模型（VLM）的新型流匹配架构，以继承互联网规模的语义知识。随后，我们讨论了如何利用来自多个灵巧机器人平台（包括单臂机器人、双臂机器人和移动操作器）的大规模多样化数据集训练该模型。我们从零样本预训练任务执行能力、遵循人类及高级VLM策略的语言指令能力，以及通过微调获取新技能的能力等方面评估了模型。我们的结果涵盖了多种任务，如叠衣服、清洁桌子和组装盒子。

contrib.en: Proposes π0, a novel vision-language-action flow model for general robot control.
contrib.zh: 提出π0，一种用于通用机器人控制的新型视觉-语言-动作流模型。

contrib.en: Integrates pre-trained VLM with flow matching for high-frequency dexterous action chunks.
contrib.zh: 将预训练VLM与流匹配结合，实现高频灵巧动作块。

contrib.en: Demonstrates cross-embodiment training on 7 robot platforms with 10,000 hours of data.
contrib.zh: 在7种机器人平台上使用10,000小时数据进行跨本体训练。

contrib.en: Shows zero-shot task performance and language instruction following after pre-training.
contrib.zh: 展示预训练后的零样本任务性能和语言指令跟随能力。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on complex dexterous tasks like laundry folding and box assembly.
contrib.zh: 在叠衣服和组装盒子等复杂灵巧任务上取得最先进结果。

contrib.en: Introduces a pre-training/post-training recipe analogous to LLMs for robot foundation models.
contrib.zh: 引入类似大语言模型的预训练/后训练方案用于机器人基础模型。

limits.en: Limited understanding of optimal pre-training data composition and weighting.
limits.zh: 对最优预训练数据组成和权重的理解有限。

limits.en: Not all evaluated tasks achieve reliable performance; failure modes remain.
limits.zh: 并非所有评估任务都达到可靠性能；仍存在失败模式。

limits.en: Unclear how to predict data amount and type needed for near-perfect performance.
limits.zh: 不清楚如何预测接近完美性能所需的数据量和类型。

limits.en: Positive transfer across highly diverse domains (e.g., driving, navigation) not yet validated.
limits.zh: 跨高度多样化领域（如驾驶、导航）的正向迁移尚未验证。

limits.en: Model size and computational cost may limit real-time deployment on resource-constrained robots.
limits.zh: 模型大小和计算成本可能限制在资源受限机器人上的实时部署。

items.en: Robot learning holds tremendous promise to unlock the full potential of flexible, general, and dexterous robot systems, as well as to address some of the deepest questions in artificial intelligence. However, bringing robot learning to the level of generality required for effective real-world systems faces major obstacles in terms of data, generalization, and robustness. In this paper, we discuss how generalist robot policies (i.e., robot foundation models) can address these challenges, and how we can design effective generalist robot policies for complex and highly dexterous tasks. We propose a novel flow matching architecture built on top of a pre-trained vision-language model (VLM) to inherit Internet-scale semantic knowledge. We then discuss how this model can be trained on a large and diverse dataset from multiple dexterous robot platforms, including single-arm robots, dual-arm robots, and mobile manipulators. We evaluate our model in terms of its ability to perform tasks in zero shot after pre-training, follow language instructions from people and from a high-level VLM policy, and its ability to acquire new skills via fine-tuning. Our results cover a wide variety of tasks, such as laundry folding, table cleaning, and assembling boxes.
items.zh: 机器人学习在释放灵活、通用和灵巧机器人系统的全部潜力，以及解决人工智能中一些最深层次问题方面，具有巨大的前景。然而，将机器人学习提升到有效现实世界系统所需的通用性水平，在数据、泛化和鲁棒性方面面临重大障碍。在本文中，我们讨论了通用机器人策略（即机器人基础模型）如何应对这些挑战，以及如何为复杂且高度灵巧的任务设计有效的通用机器人策略。我们提出了一种新颖的流匹配架构，该架构构建在预训练的视觉语言模型（VLM）之上，以继承互联网规模的语义知识。然后，我们讨论了如何利用来自多个灵巧机器人平台（包括单臂机器人、双臂机器人和移动操作器）的大规模多样化数据集来训练该模型。我们评估了模型在预训练后零样本执行任务、遵循人类和高级VLM策略的语言指令，以及通过微调获取新技能的能力。我们的结果涵盖了多种任务，例如叠衣服、清理桌子和组装盒子。

items.capEn: Figure 2: 𝝅𝟎subscript𝝅0\bm{\pi_{0}} controls a mobile manipulator to fold laundry. Our model is pre-trained on diverse data from 7 distinct robot configurations and 68 tasks, and can then be used in zero-shot or fine-tuned to complex downstream tasks, as in the case of this laundry folding policy, which fetches laundry from a dryer, packs it into a hamper, brings the hamper to a folding table, and then folds each article of clothing.
items.capZh: 图2：π0控制移动操作器叠衣服。我们的模型在来自7种不同机器人配置和68个任务的多样化数据上进行了预训练，然后可以零样本使用或微调以完成复杂的下游任务，如本例中的叠衣服策略，该策略从烘干机中取出衣物，将其装入洗衣篮，将洗衣篮带到折叠桌，然后折叠每件衣物。

items.en: A human being should be able to change a diaper, plan an invasion, butcher a hog, conn a ship, design a building, write a sonnet, balance accounts, build a wall, set a bone, comfort the dying, take orders, give orders, cooperate, act alone, solve equations, analyze a new problem, pitch manure, program a computer, cook a tasty meal, fight efficiently, die gallantly. Specialization is for insects.
items.zh: 一个人应该能够换尿布、策划入侵、宰杀猪、驾驶船只、设计建筑、写十四行诗、平衡账目、砌墙、接骨、安慰临终者、接受命令、下达命令、合作、单独行动、解方程、分析新问题、施肥、编程、烹饪美味佳肴、高效战斗、英勇赴死。专业化是昆虫的事。

items.en: Artificial intelligence systems come in all shapes and sizes, from highly specialized systems that solve complex problems inaccessible to the human mind, such as predicting the conformation of a protein [21], to systems that can produce lifelike high-resolution images or videos based on textual prompts [40]. However, the axis along which human intelligence most outpaces machine intelligence is versatility: the ability to solve diverse tasks situated in varied physical environments, while responding intelligently to environmental constraints, language commands, and unexpected perturbations. Perhaps the most tangible progress toward this kind of versatility in AI can be seen in large language- and vision-language models [1, 48]: systems that are pre-trained on large and very diverse corpora of images and text from the web, and then fine-tuned (“aligned”) using more carefully curated datasets meant to induce the desired pattern of behavior and responsiveness. While such models have been shown to exhibit broad instruction-following and problem-solving abilities [53, 27], they are not truly situated in a physical world the way that people are, and their understanding of physical interaction is based entirely on abstract descriptions. If such methods are to make tangible progress toward AI systems that exhibit the kind of physically situated versatility that people possess, we will need to train them on physically situated data — that is, data from embodied robot agents.
items.zh: 人工智能系统形态各异，从解决人类思维无法企及的复杂问题的高度专业化系统（例如预测蛋白质构象[21]），到能够根据文本提示生成逼真高分辨率图像或视频的系统[40]。然而，人类智能最远超机器智能的维度是通用性：解决位于各种物理环境中的多样化任务的能力，同时智能地响应环境约束、语言命令和意外扰动。在AI中，朝着这种通用性最显著的进展或许可以在大型语言模型和视觉语言模型[1, 48]中看到：这些系统在来自网络的大规模且高度多样化的图像和文本语料库上进行预训练，然后使用更精心策划的数据集进行微调（“对齐”），以诱导出期望的行为模式和响应能力。虽然此类模型已展现出广泛的指令遵循和问题解决能力[53, 27]，但它们并不像人类那样真正置身于物理世界中，它们对物理交互的理解完全基于抽象描述。如果这些方法要在展现人类所拥有的那种物理情境通用性的AI系统方面取得实质性进展，我们将需要在物理情境数据——即来自具身机器人智能体的数据——上训练它们。

items.en: Flexible and general-purpose models that can be tasked to perform a variety of robot behaviors have tremendous practical ramifications, but they may also offer solutions to some of the toughest challenges facing robot learning today, such as availability of data, generalization, and robustness. In natural language [1] and computer vision [39], general-purpose foundation models that are pre-trained on diverse multi-task data tend to outperform narrowly tailored and specialized solutions. For example, if the goal is to recognize birds in photographs, it is likely more expedient to pre-train on many different image-language associations and then fine-tune or prompt for the bird recognition task, than it is to train on only bird recognition data. Similarly, we may find that for effective specialized robot systems, it is more effective to first pre-train on highly diverse robot data, and then fine-tune or prompt for the desired task. This can resolve the data scarcity challenge, because many more sources of data are available to a generalist model — including data from other tasks, other robots, or even non-robot sources — and it may resolve robustness and generalization challenges, because the diverse data exhibits a greater coverage of observations and actions, providing a variety of scenes, corrections, and recovery behaviors that might not be present in more narrow specialized data. Thus, adopting a large-scale pre-training approach to robot learning has the potential to address many of the field’s challenges and make practical learning-enabled robots a reality, while at the same time furthering our understanding of the deepest problems in artificial intelligence.
items.zh: 灵活且通用的模型，能够被指派执行各种机器人行为，具有巨大的实际意义，但它们也可能为当今机器人学习面临的一些最严峻挑战（如数据可用性、泛化和鲁棒性）提供解决方案。在自然语言[1]和计算机视觉[39]中，在多样化多任务数据上预训练的通用基础模型往往优于狭隘定制和专门化的解决方案。例如，如果目标是识别照片中的鸟类，更有效的方法可能是先在许多不同的图像-语言关联上进行预训练，然后针对鸟类识别任务进行微调或提示，而不是仅使用鸟类识别数据进行训练。类似地，我们可能会发现，对于有效的专用机器人系统，首先在高度多样化的机器人数据上进行预训练，然后针对所需任务进行微调或提示更为有效。这可以解决数据稀缺的挑战，因为通用模型可以获得更多数据源——包括来自其他任务、其他机器人甚至非机器人源的数据——并且可能解决鲁棒性和泛化挑战，因为多样化数据提供了更广泛的观测和动作覆盖，提供了更狭窄的专用数据中可能不存在的各种场景、纠正和恢复行为。因此，对机器人学习采用大规模预训练方法有可能解决该领域的许多挑战，使实用的学习型机器人成为现实，同时加深我们对人工智能中最深层次问题的理解。

items.en: However, developing such generalist robot policies — i.e., robot foundation models — involves a number of major challenges. First, any such research must be done at a very large scale, because the full benefits of large-scale pre-training are often not present at smaller scales [54]. Second, it requires developing the right model architectures that can effectively make use of diverse data sources, while at the same time being able to represent the intricate and subtle behaviors necessary to interact with complex physical scenes. Third, it requires the right training recipe. This is perhaps the most important ingredient, as much of the recent progress with large models in NLP and computer vision has relied heavily on delicate strategies for curating pre-training and post-training data [35].
items.zh: 然而，开发这种通用机器人策略——即机器人基础模型——涉及许多重大挑战。首先，任何此类研究都必须在大规模上进行，因为大规模预训练的全部好处通常在小规模下并不显现[54]。其次，需要开发正确的模型架构，既能有效利用多样化数据源，又能表示与复杂物理场景交互所必需的精细微妙行为。第三，需要正确的训练配方。这或许是最重要的因素，因为NLP和计算机视觉中大型模型的最新进展很大程度上依赖于精心策划预训练和后训练数据的策略[35]。

items.en: In this paper, we present a prototype model and learning framework, which we call π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}, that illustrates how each of these three bottlenecks could be tackled. We illustrate our model and system in Figure LABEL:fig:teaser. To incorporate diverse data sources, we begin by utilizing a pre-trained vision-language model (VLM) to import Internet-scale experience. By basing our model on a VLM, we inherit the general knowledge, semantic reasoning, and problem-solving abilities of language- and vision-language models. We then further train our model to incorporate robot actions, turning it into a vision-language-action (VLA) model [7]. In order to make it feasible to utilize a variety of diverse robot data sources, we employ cross-embodiment training [10], where data from many robot types is combined into the same model. These different robot types have different configuration spaces and action representations, including single and dual-arm systems, as well as mobile manipulators. Additionally, in order to make it possible to perform highly dexterous and intricate physical tasks, we use an action chunking architecture [57] with flow matching (a variant of diffusion) to represent complex continuous action distributions [28, 32]. This enables our model to control robots at frequencies of up to 50 Hz for dexterous tasks such as laundry folding (see Figure LABEL:fig:teaser). To combine flow matching with VLMs, we use a novel action expert that augments the standard VLM with flow-based outputs.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一个原型模型和学习框架，称为π0，它说明了如何应对这三个瓶颈。我们在图LABEL:fig:teaser中展示了我们的模型和系统。为了整合多样化数据源，我们首先利用预训练的视觉语言模型（VLM）导入互联网规模的经验。通过将模型基于VLM，我们继承了语言模型和视觉语言模型的通用知识、语义推理和问题解决能力。然后，我们进一步训练模型以融入机器人动作，将其转变为视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型[7]。为了能够利用各种不同的机器人数据源，我们采用了跨本体训练[10]，将来自多种机器人类型的数据合并到同一个模型中。这些不同的机器人类型具有不同的配置空间和动作表示，包括单臂和双臂系统，以及移动操作器。此外，为了能够执行高度灵巧和精细的物理任务，我们使用了带有流匹配（扩散的一种变体）的动作分块架构[57]来表示复杂的连续动作分布[28, 32]。这使得我们的模型能够以高达50 Hz的频率控制机器人执行灵巧任务，例如叠衣服（见图LABEL:fig:teaser）。为了将流匹配与VLM结合，我们使用了一个新颖的动作专家，它用基于流的输出增强了标准VLM。

items.en: As with language models, the architecture of our model is only part of our method. In order to flexibly and robustly perform complex tasks, we need the right training recipe. Our recipe mirrors the pre-training/post-training separation commonly seen in exascale language- and image-language models [1, 48], where the model is first pre-trained on a very large and diverse corpus, and then fine-tuned on more narrow and more carefully curated data to induce the desired pattern of behavior — in our case, dexterity, efficiency, and robustness. Intuitively, training only on high-quality data does not teach the model how to recover from mistakes, since mistakes are rarely seen in such data. Training on only lower-quality pre-training data does not teach the model to act efficiently and robustly. Combining both provides the desired behavior: the model attempts insofar as possible to act in a manner similar to the high-quality data, but still has a repertoire of recoveries and corrections that it can deploy in the case of a mistake.
items.zh: 与语言模型一样，我们模型的架构只是方法的一部分。为了灵活且鲁棒地执行复杂任务，我们需要正确的训练配方。我们的配方模仿了超大规模语言模型和图像语言模型中常见的预训练/后训练分离[1, 48]，其中模型首先在非常大且多样化的语料库上进行预训练，然后在更狭窄且更精心策划的数据上进行微调，以诱导出期望的行为模式——在我们的案例中，是灵巧性、效率和鲁棒性。直观地说，仅在高品质数据上训练并不能教会模型如何从错误中恢复，因为此类数据中很少出现错误。仅在较低质量的预训练数据上训练则不能教会模型高效且鲁棒地行动。两者结合提供了期望的行为：模型尽可能以类似于高品质数据的方式行动，但仍然拥有在出错时可以部署的恢复和纠正技能。

items.en: The contributions of our work consist of a novel generalist robot policy architecture based on VLM pre-training and flow matching, and an empirical investigation of pre-training/post-training recipes for such robot foundation models. We evaluate our model for zero-shot control with language commands, with fine-tuning to downstream tasks, and in combination with a high-level semantic policy that outputs intermediate language commands to perform complex and temporally extended tasks. While our model and system make use of a variety of ideas presented in recent work, the combination of ingredients is novel, and the empirical evaluation demonstrates a level of dexterity and generality that goes significantly beyond previously demonstrated robot foundation models. We evaluate our approach by pre-training on over 10,000 hours of robot data, and fine-tuning to a variety of dexterous tasks, including laundry folding (see Figure 2), clearing a table, putting dishes in a microwave, stacking eggs into a carton, assembling a box, and bagging groceries.
items.zh: 我们工作的贡献包括一种基于VLM预训练和流匹配的新颖通用机器人策略架构，以及对此类机器人基础模型的预训练/后训练配方的实证研究。我们评估了我们的模型在语言命令下的零样本控制、对下游任务的微调，以及与输出中间语言命令以执行复杂且时间上扩展任务的高级语义策略的结合。虽然我们的模型和系统利用了近期工作中提出的各种想法，但这些成分的组合是新颖的，实证评估展示的灵巧性和通用性水平显著超越了先前展示的机器人基础模型。我们通过在超过10,000小时的机器人数据上进行预训练，并微调到各种灵巧任务（包括叠衣服（见图2）、清理桌子、将盘子放入微波炉、将鸡蛋堆叠到纸盒中、组装盒子和装袋杂货）来评估我们的方法。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Overview of our framework. We start with a pre-training mixture, which consists of both our own dexterous manipulation datasets and open-source data. We use this mixture to train our flow matching VLA model, which consists of a larger VLM backbone and a smaller action expert for processing robot states and actions. The VLM backbone weights are initialized from PaliGemma [5], providing representations learned from large-scale Internet pre-training. The resulting π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} model can be used to control multiple robot embodiments with differing action spaces to accomplish a wide variety of tasks.
items.capZh: 图3：我们框架的概述。我们从预训练混合开始，该混合包括我们自己的灵巧操作数据集和开源数据。我们使用这个混合来训练我们的流匹配VLA模型，该模型由一个较大的VLM主干和一个较小的用于处理机器人状态和动作的动作专家组成。VLM主干权重从PaliGemma [5]初始化，提供了从大规模互联网预训练中学到的表示。得到的π0模型可用于控制具有不同动作空间的多个机器人本体，以完成各种任务。

items.en: Our work builds on recently proposed methods in large-scale robot learning, as well as multimodal language models. Our work is most closely related to recently proposed vision-language action (VLA) models, which use pre-trained VLMs that are fine-tuned for robot control [7, 24, 55]. Such models employ autoregressive discretization to represent actions in a manner analogous to text tokens. In contrast, our model employs a novel design that fine-tunes a VLM to produce actions via flow matching [32, 28], a variant of diffusion [20, 46]. This allows us to handle high-frequency action chunks [57] (up to 50 Hz) and highly dexterous tasks, which we show pose a major challenge for prior autoregressive VLAs [7]. This resembles a number of recent works on diffusion models for action generation [9, 60]. In contrast to these works, our model uses a pre-trained VLM backbone [5]. Our contribution is also fundamentally integrative, focusing on a framework for robot foundation models, including not only the model architecture itself but also a pre-training recipe, pre-training and post-training phases, and a range of real-world experiments.
items.zh: 我们的工作建立在最近提出的大规模机器人学习方法以及多模态语言模型的基础上。我们的工作与最近提出的视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型最为相关，这些模型使用预训练的VLM进行微调以用于机器人控制[7, 24, 55]。此类模型采用自回归离散化以类似于文本标记的方式表示动作。相比之下，我们的模型采用了一种新颖的设计，通过流匹配[32, 28]（扩散的一种变体[20, 46]）微调VLM以生成动作。这使我们能够处理高频动作块[57]（高达50 Hz）和高度灵巧的任务，我们证明这对先前的自回归VLA构成了重大挑战[7]。这类似于近期关于动作生成扩散模型的许多工作[9, 60]。与这些工作相比，我们的模型使用了预训练的VLM主干[5]。我们的贡献从根本上也是整合性的，专注于机器人基础模型的框架，不仅包括模型架构本身，还包括预训练配方、预训练和后训练阶段，以及一系列真实世界实验。

items.en: Outside of robot control, many models have been proposed that combine pre-trained language models with diffusion [40, 41, 14], including models that specifically hybridize diffusion and autoregressive large language models [19, 29, 59]. Such models are typically concerned with image generation, but our action generation model builds on a number of previously proposed concepts. Like Zhou et al. [59], we train our model via a diffusion-style (flow matching) loss applied on individual sequence elements, in lieu of the standard cross-entropy loss for decoder-only transformers. Like Liu et al. [29], we use a separate set of weights for the tokens corresponding to diffusion. Incorporating these concepts into a VLA model, we introduce what to our knowledge is the first flow matching VLA that produces high-frequency action chunks for dexterous control.
items.zh: 在机器人控制之外，许多模型已经提出将预训练语言模型与扩散相结合[40, 41, 14]，包括专门混合扩散和自回归大型语言模型的模型[19, 29, 59]。此类模型通常关注图像生成，但我们的动作生成模型建立在许多先前提出的概念之上。与Zhou等人[59]类似，我们通过应用于单个序列元素的扩散风格（流匹配）损失来训练模型，以替代仅解码器Transformer的标准交叉熵损失。与Liu等人[29]类似，我们对对应于扩散的标记使用一组单独的权重。将这些概念融入VLA模型，我们引入了据我们所知第一个为灵巧控制生成高频动作块的流匹配VLA。

items.en: Our work also builds on a rich history of prior works on large-scale robot learning. Early work in this area often utilized self-supervised or autonomous data collection [26, 22, 8], providing a tractable data source for simple tasks such as grasping [18, 37] or pushing [56], but without the complexity of more dexterous behaviors. More recently, a number of high-quality datasets have been collected for robot control that enable broad generalization [23, 10, 52, 33, 34, 43, 13, 6], but typically for simpler tasks that consist of object relocation and rudimentary furniture manipulation (e.g., drawer opening) [31, 15]. More dexterous tasks have been studied at a smaller scale, typically with 10s or 100s of training trajectories [57], equivalent to 10 or less hours. Since one of our aims is to study complex and dexterous behaviors, we utilize a much larger dataset, with about 10,000 hours of demonstrations, complemented by the open-source OXE dataset [10]. To our knowledge, this represents by far the largest robot learning experiment in terms of the amount of robot data. At this scale, we show that a more sophisticated pre-training/post-training recipe is highly effective — analogously to the recipes used for large language models, a pre-training phase endows our model with a broad base of knowledge, which is then refined in a post-training phase with higher-quality curated data to achieve the desired behavior.
items.zh: 我们的工作也建立在先前大规模机器人学习工作的丰富历史之上。该领域的早期工作通常利用自监督或自主数据收集[26, 22, 8]，为抓取[18, 37]或推动[56]等简单任务提供了可行的数据源，但没有更灵巧行为的复杂性。最近，已经收集了许多用于机器人控制的高质量数据集，这些数据集实现了广泛的泛化[23, 10, 52, 33, 34, 43, 13, 6]，但通常针对由物体重新定位和基本家具操作（例如打开抽屉）组成的更简单任务[31, 15]。更灵巧的任务已在较小规模上研究，通常有数十或数百个训练轨迹[57]，相当于10小时或更少。由于我们的目标之一是研究复杂和灵巧的行为，我们使用了更大的数据集，大约有10,000小时的演示，并辅以开源OXE数据集[10]。据我们所知，就机器人数据量而言，这代表了迄今为止最大的机器人学习实验。在这个规模上，我们展示了更复杂的预训练/后训练配方非常有效——类似于用于大型语言模型的配方，预训练阶段赋予模型广泛的知识基础，然后在后训练阶段使用更高质量的策划数据进行精炼，以实现期望的行为。

items.en: The complexity of the tasks we illustrate goes significantly beyond prior work. While recent work has illustrated a number of more complex and dexterous behaviors, such as tying shoelaces [58] or cooking shrimp [17], we show that our framework can train very long tasks, sometimes tens of minutes in length, for behaviors that combine both physical dexterity and combinatorial complexity. For example, our laundry folding task requires the robot to manipulate a variety of clothing items that can start in any configuration, and fold multiple items in sequence. Our table bussing task requires discerning the class of novel objects (trash or dishes). We show that a single cross-embodiment model can be used as the base model for these tasks. To our knowledge, our work demonstrates the longest dexterous tasks in the end-to-end robot learning literature.
items.zh: 我们展示的任务的复杂性显著超越了先前的工作。虽然近期工作展示了许多更复杂和灵巧的行为，例如系鞋带[58]或烹饪虾[17]，但我们表明我们的框架可以训练非常长的任务，有时长达数十分钟，这些行为结合了物理灵巧性和组合复杂性。例如，我们的叠衣服任务要求机器人操作各种可以以任何配置开始的衣物，并顺序折叠多个物品。我们的餐桌清理任务要求辨别新物体的类别（垃圾或餐具）。我们表明，单个跨本体模型可以用作这些任务的基础模型。据我们所知，我们的工作展示了端到端机器人学习文献中最长的灵巧任务。

items.en: We provide an outline of our model and training procedure in Figure 3. In our training framework, we first assemble a pre-training mixture consisting of a weighted combination of our own dexterous manipulation datasets (Section V-C), collected on 7 different robot configurations for 68 different tasks, and the entire OXE dataset [10], which contains data from 22 robots. The pre-training phase (Section V-A) also uses diverse language labels, combining task names and segment annotations (fine-grained labels for sub-trajectories, typically about 2 seconds in length). The purpose of the pre-training phase is to train a base model that exhibits broad capabilities and generalization, but is not necessarily specialized for high performance on any one task. This base model can follow language commands and perform a variety of tasks at rudimentary proficiency. For complex and dexterous tasks, we then employ a post-training procedure (Section V-A), which uses high-quality curated data to adapt the model to specific downstream tasks. We study both efficient post-training with small to moderate amounts of data, and high-quality post-training with larger datasets for complex tasks such as laundry folding and mobile manipulation.
items.zh: 我们在图3中提供了我们的模型和训练程序的概述。在我们的训练框架中，我们首先组装一个预训练混合，包括我们自己的灵巧操作数据集（第V-C节）的加权组合（在7种不同机器人配置上收集的68个不同任务）以及整个OXE数据集[10]（包含来自22个机器人的数据）。预训练阶段（第V-A节）也使用多样化的语言标签，结合任务名称和片段注释（子轨迹的细粒度标签，通常约2秒长）。预训练阶段的目的是训练一个具有广泛能力和泛化能力的基础模型，但不一定在任何单一任务上专门化以实现高性能。这个基础模型可以遵循语言命令并以基本熟练程度执行各种任务。对于复杂和灵巧的任务，我们随后采用后训练程序（第V-A节），该程序使用高质量策划数据将模型适应特定的下游任务。我们研究了使用少量到中等数据的高效后训练，以及针对复杂任务（如叠衣服和移动操作）使用更大数据集的高质量后训练。

items.en: Our model, which we describe in Section IV, is based on the PaliGemma vision-language model [5], which we then further train with our data mixture. To turn the base PaliGemma VLM into π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}, we add action outputs that use flow matching [32, 28] to generate continuous action distributions. We describe this design in detail in the following section. Note that we use PaliGemma for convenience and because of its comparatively small size (which is useful for real-time control), but our framework is compatible with any base pre-trained VLM.
items.zh: 我们的模型（在第IV节中描述）基于PaliGemma视觉语言模型[5]，我们随后使用我们的数据混合对其进行进一步训练。为了将基础PaliGemma VLM转变为π0，我们添加了使用流匹配[32, 28]生成连续动作分布的动作输出。我们在下一节中详细描述这一设计。请注意，我们为了方便以及其相对较小的尺寸（这对于实时控制很有用）而使用PaliGemma，但我们的框架与任何基础预训练VLM兼容。

items.en: The π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} model, illustrated in Figure 3, consists primarily of a language model transformer backbone. Following the standard late fusion VLM recipe [3, 11, 30], image encoders embed the robot’s image observations into the same embedding space as language tokens. We further augment this backbone with robotics-specific inputs and outputs — namely, proprioceptive state and robot actions. π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} uses conditional flow matching [28, 32] to model the continuous distribution of actions. Flow matching provides our model with high precision and multimodal modeling capability, making it especially well suited to high-frequency dexterous tasks. Our architecture is inspired by Transfusion [59], which trains a single transformer using multiple objectives, with tokens111In this paper, we use the word “token” to refer to an input/output slot along the sequence dimension, whether the slot corresponds to a discrete variable (e.g., a language token) or a continuous variable (e.g., an image patch or a robot action). corresponding to continuous outputs supervised via a flow matching loss and tokens corresponding to discrete outputs supervised via a cross-entropy loss. Building on Transfusion, we additionally found that using a separate set of weights for the robotics-specific (action and state) tokens led to an improvement in performance. This design is analogous to a mixture of experts [45, 25, 12, 16] with two mixture elements, where the first element is used for image and text inputs, and the second is used for robotics-specific inputs and outputs. We refer to the second set of weights as the action expert.
items.zh: π0模型（如图3所示）主要由一个语言模型Transformer主干组成。遵循标准的晚期融合VLM配方[3, 11, 30]，图像编码器将机器人的图像观测嵌入到与语言标记相同的嵌入空间中。我们进一步用机器人特定的输入和输出——即本体感受状态和机器人动作——增强这个主干。π0使用条件流匹配[28, 32]来建模动作的连续分布。流匹配为我们的模型提供了高精度和多模态建模能力，使其特别适合高频灵巧任务。我们的架构受到Transfusion [59]的启发，该模型使用多个目标训练单个Transformer，其中对应于连续输出的标记通过流匹配损失进行监督，对应于离散输出的标记通过交叉熵损失进行监督。在Transfusion的基础上，我们还发现对机器人特定（动作和状态）标记使用一组单独的权重可以提高性能。这种设计类似于具有两个混合元素的专家混合[45, 25, 12, 16]，其中第一个元素用于图像和文本输入，第二个用于机器人特定的输入和输出。我们将第二组权重称为动作专家。

Note that inference can be implemented efficiently by caching the attention keys and values for the prefix 𝐨tsubscript𝐨𝑡\mathbf{o}_{t} and only recomputing the suffix corresponding to the action tokens for each integration step. items.en: While in principle our model can be initialized from scratch or fine-tuned from any VLM backbone, in practice we use PaliGemma [5] as our base model. PaliGemma is an open-source 3 billion parameter VLM that offers a convenient tradeoff between size and performance. We add 300M parameters for the action expert (which is initialized from scratch) for a total of 3.3 billion parameters. We provide a full description of the model architecture in Appendix A-B.
items.zh: 虽然原则上我们的模型可以从头开始初始化或从任何 VLM 主干微调，但实践中我们使用 PaliGemma [5] 作为基础模型。PaliGemma 是一个开源的 30 亿参数 VLM，在规模和性能之间提供了方便的权衡。我们为动作专家（从头初始化）添加了 3 亿参数，总计 33 亿参数。我们在附录 A-B 中提供了模型架构的完整描述。

items.en: Non-VLM baseline model. In addition to our main VLA model, we also trained a similar baseline model that did not use a VLM initialization for ablation experiments. This model, which we refer to as π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small, has 470M parameters, does not use VLM initialization, and has a number of small differences that we found to be helpful for training on our data without VLM initialization, which are summarized in Appendix A-C. This model is used in our comparisons to evaluate the benefits of incorporating VLM pertaining.
items.zh: 非 VLM 基线模型。除了我们的主要 VLA 模型外，我们还训练了一个类似的基线模型，该模型未使用 VLM 初始化用于消融实验。这个模型，我们称之为 π0-small，有 4.7 亿参数，不使用 VLM 初始化，并且有一些小的差异，我们发现这些差异有助于在没有 VLM 初始化的情况下在我们的数据上进行训练，这些差异总结在附录 A-C 中。该模型用于我们的比较中，以评估结合 VLM 预训练的好处。

full.sec: V Data Collection and Training Recipe
full.secZh: 五、数据收集与训练方案

items.en: Broadly capable robot foundation models require not only an expressive and powerful architecture, but also the right dataset and, more importantly, the right training recipe. In the same way that LLM training is typically divided into pre-training and post-training phases, we employ a multi-stage training procedure for our model. The goal of the pre-training phase is to expose the model to a diverse range of tasks so that it can acquire broadly applicable and general physical capabilities, while the goal of the post-training phase is to provide the model with the ability to skillfully and fluently execute the desired downstream task. Because of this, the requirements for the pre-training and post-training datasets are distinct: the pre-training dataset should cover as many tasks as possible, and within each of those tasks should cover a diversity of behaviors. The post-training dataset should instead cover behaviors that are conducive to effective task execution, which should exhibit a consistent and fluent strategy. Intuitively, the diverse (but lower quality) pre-training data allows the model to recover from mistakes and handle highly varied situations, which might not otherwise occur in the high-quality post-training data, while the post-training data teaches the model to perform the task well.
items.zh: 广泛能力的机器人基础模型不仅需要表达能力强且强大的架构，还需要正确的数据集，更重要的是，正确的训练方法。就像 LLM 训练通常分为预训练和后训练阶段一样，我们为模型采用了多阶段训练程序。预训练阶段的目标是让模型接触多样化的任务，使其获得广泛适用且通用的物理能力，而后训练阶段的目标是为模型提供熟练且流畅执行所需下游任务的能力。因此，对预训练和后训练数据集的要求是不同的：预训练数据集应尽可能覆盖更多的任务，并且在每个任务中应涵盖多样化的行为。后训练数据集则应涵盖有助于有效任务执行的行为，这些行为应表现出一致且流畅的策略。直观地说，多样化（但质量较低）的预训练数据使模型能够从错误中恢复并处理高度变化的情况，而这些情况可能不会出现在高质量的后训练数据中，而后训练数据则教会模型如何很好地执行任务。

full.secZh: 五-A 预训练与后训练

items.capEn: Figure 4: Overview of our dataset: The pre-training mixture consists of a subset of OXE [10] and the π𝜋\pi dataset. We use a subset of OXE, which we refer to as OXE Magic Soup [24]. The right figure illustrates the weight of the different datasets in the pre-training mixture. The left figure illustrates their relative sizes as measured by the number of steps.
items.capZh: 图 4：数据集概览：预训练混合数据包括 OXE [10] 的子集和 π 数据集。我们使用 OXE 的一个子集，称为 OXE Magic Soup [24]。右图说明了预训练混合数据中不同数据集的权重。左图说明了它们按步数衡量的相对大小。

items.en: We provide an overview of our pre-training mixture in Figure 4. Since each training example corresponds to a timestep — i.e., a tuple (𝐨t,𝐀t)subscript𝐨𝑡subscript𝐀𝑡(\mathbf{o}_{t},\mathbf{A}_{t}), — we will quantify data in terms of timesteps in this discussion. 9.1%percent9.19.1\% of the training mixture consists of open-source datasets, including OXE [10], Bridge v2 [52], and DROID [23]. The robots and tasks in these datasets typically have one or two cameras and use low-frequency control, between 2 and 10 Hz. However, these datasets cover a wide range of objects and environments. To learn dexterous and more complex tasks, we also use 903M timesteps of data from our own datasets, where 106M steps are from single-arm robots and 797M are from dual-arm robots. This data has 68 tasks, where each task is composed of complex behaviors — e.g., the “bussing” task involves putting a wide range of different dishes, cups, and utensils into a bussing bin, and a wide array of trash items into the garbage. Note that this definition of task is significantly different from prior work, which typically uses any combination of noun and verb (e.g., “pick up the cup” vs. “pick up the plate”) to constitute a distinct task. Therefore, the actual range of behaviors in our dataset is significantly broader than this number of “tasks” would imply. We discuss the specific robots and tasks in our dataset in more detail in Section V-C.
items.zh: 我们在图 4 中提供了预训练混合数据的概览。由于每个训练样本对应一个时间步——即元组 (𝐨t, 𝐀t)——我们将在本讨论中以时间步为单位量化数据。训练混合数据中 9.1% 来自开源数据集，包括 OXE [10]、Bridge v2 [52] 和 DROID [23]。这些数据集中的机器人和任务通常有一个或两个摄像头，并使用低频控制，频率在 2 到 10 Hz 之间。然而，这些数据集涵盖了广泛的物体和环境。为了学习灵巧且更复杂的任务，我们还使用了来自我们自己数据集的 9.03 亿时间步数据，其中 1.06 亿步来自单臂机器人，7.97 亿步来自双臂机器人。这些数据包含 68 个任务，每个任务由复杂的行为组成——例如，“清理餐桌”任务涉及将各种不同的盘子、杯子和餐具放入清理箱，以及将各种垃圾物品放入垃圾桶。请注意，这个任务定义与先前的工作显著不同，先前的工作通常使用名词和动词的任何组合（例如，“拿起杯子”与“拿起盘子”）来构成一个不同的任务。因此，我们数据集中的实际行为范围比这个“任务”数量所暗示的要广泛得多。我们在第 V-C 节中更详细地讨论了具体机器人和任务。

items.en: Since the datasets are somewhat imbalanced in size (e.g., the more difficult laundry folding tasks are overrepresented), we weight each task-robot combination by n0.43superscript𝑛0.43n^{0.43}, where n𝑛n is the number of samples for that combination, such that over-represented combinations are down-weighted. The configuration vector 𝐪tsubscript𝐪𝑡\mathbf{q}_{t} and action vectors 𝐚tsubscript𝐚𝑡\mathbf{a}_{t} always have the dimensionality of the largest robot in the dataset (18 in our case, to accommodate two 6-DoF arms, 2 grippers, a mobile base, and a vertically actuated torso). For robots with lower-dimensional configuration and action spaces, we zero-pad the configuration and action vectors. For robots with fewer than three images, we also mask out the missing image slots.
items.zh: 由于数据集在大小上有些不平衡（例如，更难的叠衣服任务占比过高），我们按 n^0.43 对每个任务-机器人组合进行加权，其中 n 是该组合的样本数，以便过度代表的组合被降低权重。配置向量 𝐪t 和动作向量 𝐚t 始终具有数据集中最大机器人的维度（在我们的例子中为 18，以适应两个 6 自由度手臂、2 个夹爪、一个移动底座和一个垂直驱动的躯干）。对于配置和动作空间维度较低的机器人，我们对配置和动作向量进行零填充。对于图像少于三个的机器人，我们还屏蔽缺失的图像槽位。

items.en: In the post-training phase, we fine-tune our model with a smaller task-specific dataset to specialize it to particular downstream applications. As mentioned previously, our definition of “task” is fairly broad — e.g., the “bussing” task requires manipulating a wide range of different objects. Different tasks require very different datasets, with the simplest of the tasks necessitating only 5 hours and the most complex tasks using 100 or more hours of data.
items.zh: 在后训练阶段，我们使用较小的任务特定数据集对模型进行微调，使其专门用于特定的下游应用。如前所述，我们对“任务”的定义相当宽泛——例如，“清理餐桌”任务需要操作各种不同的物体。不同的任务需要非常不同的数据集，最简单的任务仅需 5 小时，而最复杂的任务则需要 100 小时或更多的数据。

full.secZh: 五-B 语言与高层策略

items.en: More complex tasks that require semantic reasoning and high-level strategy, such as table bussing, can also benefit from a high-level policy that decomposes high-level tasks (such as “bus the table”) into more immediate subtasks (such as “pick up the napkin” or “throw the napkin into the trash”). Since our model is trained to process language inputs, we can use a high-level VLM to make these semantic inferences, a method that is analogous to LLM/VLM planning methods such as SayCan [2]. We use such a high-level policy to assist our model with high-level strategy for several of our experimental tasks, as we will discuss in Section VI.
items.zh: 更复杂的任务需要语义推理和高级策略，例如清理餐桌，也可以受益于将高级任务（如“清理桌子”）分解为更直接的子任务（如“拿起餐巾”或“将餐巾扔进垃圾桶”）的高级策略。由于我们的模型经过训练可以处理语言输入，我们可以使用高级 VLM 进行这些语义推理，这种方法类似于 SayCan [2] 等 LLM/VLM 规划方法。我们使用这样的高级策略来帮助我们的模型进行高级策略，用于我们的几个实验任务，我们将在第 VI 节中讨论。

full.secZh: 五-C 机器人系统细节

items.capEn: Figure 5: The robots used in our experiments. These include single and dual-arm manipulators with 6-DoF and 7-DoF arms, as well as holonomic and nonholonomic mobile manipulators. π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} is trained jointly on all of these platforms.
items.capZh: 图 5：我们实验中使用的机器人。这些包括单臂和双臂操作器，具有 6 自由度和 7 自由度手臂，以及全向和非全向移动操作器。π0 在所有平台上联合训练。

items.en: Our dexterous manipulation datasets include 7 different robot configurations and 68 tasks. We summarize these platforms in Figure 5, and discuss them below:
items.zh: 我们的灵巧操作数据集包括 7 种不同的机器人配置和 68 个任务。我们在图 5 中总结了这些平台，并在下面讨论：

items.en: UR5e. An arm with a parallel jaw gripper, with a wrist-mounted and over-the-shoulder camera, for a total of two camera images and a 7-dimensional configuration and action space.
items.zh: UR5e。一个带有平行夹爪的手臂，配有腕部和肩部摄像头，总共两个摄像头图像和一个 7 维配置和动作空间。

items.en: Bimanual UR5e. Two UR5e setups, for a total of three camera images and a 14-dimensional configuration and action space.
items.zh: 双臂 UR5e。两个 UR5e 设置，总共三个摄像头图像和一个 14 维配置和动作空间。

items.en: Franka. The Franka setup has two cameras and an 8-dimensional configuration and action space.
items.zh: Franka。Franka 设置有两个摄像头和一个 8 维配置和动作空间。

items.en: Bimanual Trossen. This setup has two 6-DoF Trossen ViperX arms in a configuration based on the ALOHA setup [4, 57], with two wrist cameras and a base camera, and a 14-dimensional configuration and action space.
items.zh: 双臂 Trossen。该设置有两个 6 自由度 Trossen ViperX 手臂，基于 ALOHA 设置 [4, 57] 的配置，带有两个腕部摄像头和一个底座摄像头，以及一个 14 维配置和动作空间。

items.en: Bimanual ARX & bimanual AgileX. This setup uses two 6-DoF arms, and supports either ARX or AgileX arms, with three cameras (two wrist and one base) and a 14-dimensional configuration and action space. This class encompasses two distinct platforms, but we categorize them together because of their similar kinematic properties.
items.zh: 双臂 ARX 和双臂 AgileX。该设置使用两个 6 自由度手臂，支持 ARX 或 AgileX 手臂，带有三个摄像头（两个腕部和一个底座）和一个 14 维配置和动作空间。此类包含两个不同的平台，但由于其相似的运动学特性，我们将它们归为一类。

items.en: Mobile Trossen & mobile ARX. This setup is based on the Mobile ALOHA [57] platform, with two 6-DoF arms on a mobile base, which are either ARX arms or Trossen ViperX arms. The nonholonomic base adds two action dimensions, for a 14-dimensional configuration and 16-dimensional action space. There are two wrist cameras and a base camera. This class encompasses two distinct platforms, but we categorize them together because of their similar kinematic properties.
items.zh: 移动 Trossen 和移动 ARX。该设置基于 Mobile ALOHA [57] 平台，带有两个 6 自由度手臂在移动底座上，这些手臂是 ARX 手臂或 Trossen ViperX 手臂。非全向底座增加了两个动作维度，得到一个 14 维配置和 16 维动作空间。有两个腕部摄像头和一个底座摄像头。此类包含两个不同的平台，但由于其相似的运动学特性，我们将它们归为一类。

items.en: Mobile Fibocom. Two 6-DoF ARX arms on a holonomic base. The base adds three action dimensions (two for translation and one for orientation), for a 14-dimensional configuration and 17-dimensional action space.
items.zh: 移动 Fibocom。两个 6 自由度 ARX 手臂在全向底座上。底座增加了三个动作维度（两个用于平移，一个用于方向），得到一个 14 维配置和 17 维动作空间。

items.en: We summarize the proportion of our dataset from each robot in Figure 4.
items.zh: 我们在图 4 中总结了每个机器人数据集的比例。

items.en: Our experimental evaluation consists of zero-shot evaluation experiments that compare our base (pre-trained) model to alternative model designs, as well as detailed fine-tuning experiments that evaluate our model on challenging downstream tasks, comparing it to other methods that have been proposed for dexterous manipulation. We study the following research questions:
items.zh: 我们的实验评估包括零样本评估实验，将我们的基础（预训练）模型与替代模型设计进行比较，以及详细的微调实验，评估我们的模型在具有挑战性的下游任务上的表现，并与为灵巧操作提出的其他方法进行比较。我们研究以下研究问题：

items.en: How well does π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} perform after pre-training on a variety of tasks that are present in the pre-training data? We study this question by directly evaluating π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}, with comparisons to other robot foundation models.
items.zh: π0在预训练数据中的各种任务上表现如何？我们通过直接评估π0，并与其他机器人基础模型进行比较来研究这个问题。

items.en: How well does π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} follow language commands? These experiments compare π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} to π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small, a smaller version of our model without VLM initialization, to evaluate its performance on following language commands. We evaluate with both human-provided commands and commands specified by a high-level VLM policy, as discussed in Section V-B.
items.zh: π0遵循语言指令的能力如何？这些实验将π0与π0-small（一个没有VLM初始化的较小版本模型）进行比较，以评估其在遵循语言指令方面的表现。我们使用人类提供的指令和高级VLM策略指定的指令进行评估，如第V-B节所述。

items.en: How does π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} compare to methods that have been proposed specifically for addressing dexterous manipulation tasks? These experiments study downstream tasks for which we can either fine-tune our model from the pre-trained initialization, or train it from scratch on task-specific data, comparing to prior methods that were proposed for dexterous manipulation. We aim to evaluate both the benefits of our architecture and our pre-training procedure.
items.zh: π0与专门为灵巧操作任务提出的方法相比如何？这些实验研究下游任务，我们可以从预训练初始化微调模型，或从头开始在特定任务数据上训练模型，并与先前为灵巧操作提出的方法进行比较。我们旨在评估我们的架构和预训练流程的优势。

items.en: Can π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} be adapted to complex, multi-stage tasks? In our final set of experiments, we fine-tune π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} to a set of particularly complex tasks, including folding laundry and bussing a table. These tasks take between 5 and 20 minutes to complete. Some require guidance from a high-level policy.
items.zh: π0能否适应复杂的多阶段任务？在我们的最后一组实验中，我们将π0微调到一组特别复杂的任务，包括折叠衣物和清理餐桌。这些任务需要5到20分钟完成。有些需要高级策略的指导。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Zero-shot evaluation tasks: To evaluate our base model, we run it after pre-training on five tasks: shirt folding, bussing easy, bussing hard, grocery bagging, and toast out of toaster. The tasks require a combination of dexterous manipulation, multi-stage behaviors, and semantic recognition.
items.capZh: 图6：零样本评估任务：为了评估我们的基础模型，我们在预训练后直接运行它，执行五个任务：折叠衬衫、简单清理、困难清理、装袋杂货和从烤面包机中取出吐司。这些任务需要灵巧操作、多阶段行为和语义识别的结合。

items.capEn: Figure 7: Zero-shot evaluation results: We evaluate π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} trained for the full 700k steps, a version trained for 160k steps that matches the number of updates for baseline models, π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small, and three baselines: OpenVLA and Octo trained on all of our data, and OpenVLA trained only on the UR5e tasks (which we found to work better on UR5e tasks). Across all tasks and all comparisons, even the “parity” version of our model outperforms all baselines, and the full version of our model achieves the best results by a large margin.
items.capZh: 图7：零样本评估结果：我们评估了训练完整700k步的π0，训练160k步（与基线模型更新次数匹配）的版本，π0-small，以及三个基线：在所有数据上训练的OpenVLA和Octo，以及仅在UR5e任务上训练的OpenVLA（我们发现后者在UR5e任务上表现更好）。在所有任务和所有比较中，即使是“算力对等”版本的模型也优于所有基线，而完整版本的模型以较大优势取得了最佳结果。

items.en: In our first set of experiments, we evaluate the model after pre-training on our full mixture, without any post-training, to evaluate how well our base model can perform a variety of tasks. We compare to other robot foundation models in the literature: both VLAs and smaller models that are trained from scratch on the same pre-training mixture. We evaluate on the following tasks, visualized in Figure 6, with each task commanded to the same base model via a language command.
items.zh: 在我们的第一组实验中，我们在完整混合数据上预训练后直接评估模型，没有任何后训练，以评估我们的基础模型执行各种任务的能力。我们与文献中的其他机器人基础模型进行比较：包括VLA和从头开始在相同预训练混合数据上训练的较小模型。我们在以下任务上进行评估，如图6所示，每个任务通过语言指令下达给相同的基础模型。

items.en: Shirt folding: the robot must fold a t-shirt, which starts flattened.
items.zh: 折叠衬衫：机器人必须折叠一件平铺的T恤。

items.en: Bussing easy: the robot must clean a table, putting trash in the trash bin and dishes into the dish bin. The score indicates the number of objects that were placed in the correct receptacle.
items.zh: 简单清理：机器人必须清理桌子，将垃圾放入垃圾桶，餐具放入餐具箱。分数表示正确放入容器的物体数量。

items.en: Bussing hard: a harder version of the bussing task, with more objects and more challenging configurations, such as utensils intentionally placed on top of trash objects, objects obstructing each other, and some objects that are not in the pre-training dataset.
items.zh: 困难清理：清理任务的更困难版本，有更多物体和更具挑战性的配置，例如餐具故意放在垃圾物体上，物体相互遮挡，以及一些不在预训练数据集中的物体。

items.en: Grocery bagging: the robot must bag all grocery items, such as potato chips, marshmallows, and cat food.
items.zh: 装袋杂货：机器人必须将所有杂货物品装袋，例如薯片、棉花糖和猫粮。

items.en: Toast out of toaster: the robot removes toast from a toaster.
items.zh: 从烤面包机中取出吐司：机器人从烤面包机中取出吐司。

items.en: Providing comparisons for these experiments is challenging because very few prior models can operate at this scale. We compare to OpenVLA [24], a 7B parameter VLA model that was originally trained on the OXE dataset [10]. We train OpenVLA on our full mixture. This is a very difficult mixture for OpenVLA, which does not support action chunking or high-frequency control. We also compare to Octo [50], a smaller 93M parameter model. While Octo is not a VLA, it does use a diffusion process to generate actions, providing a valuable point of comparison for our flow matching VLA. We also train Octo on the same mixture as our model. Due to time constraints, we were unable to train OpenVLA and Octo for the same number of epochs as our full model. We therefore also compare to a “compute parity” version of our model, which is trained for only 160k steps (as opposed to 700k steps for our main model), which is equal to or lower than the number of steps provided to the baselines (160k for OpenVLA, 320k for Octo). We also include a version of the OpenVLA model that we fine-tuned only on the UR5e data, without cross-embodiment training, in the hopes of providing an even stronger baseline on the UR5e tasks. Finally, we include a comparison to the π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small model described in Section IV, which can be viewed as a scaled-down version of our model without VLM pre-training.
items.zh: 为这些实验提供比较具有挑战性，因为很少有先前的模型能够在这种规模下运行。我们与OpenVLA [24]（一个7B参数的VLA模型，最初在OXE数据集[10]上训练）进行比较。我们在完整混合数据上训练OpenVLA。这对OpenVLA来说是一个非常困难的混合数据，因为它不支持动作分块或高频控制。我们还与Octo [50]（一个较小的93M参数模型）进行比较。虽然Octo不是VLA，但它使用扩散过程生成动作，为我们的流匹配VLA提供了有价值的比较点。我们也在与我们的模型相同的混合数据上训练Octo。由于时间限制，我们无法将OpenVLA和Octo训练到与我们的完整模型相同的轮数。因此，我们还与我们的模型的“算力对等”版本进行比较，该版本仅训练了160k步（而我们的主要模型训练了700k步），这等于或低于提供给基线的步数（OpenVLA为160k，Octo为320k）。我们还包含一个仅在UR5e数据上微调的OpenVLA模型版本，没有跨本体训练，希望为UR5e任务提供更强的基线。最后，我们与第IV节描述的π0-small模型进行比较，该模型可以视为没有VLM预训练的缩小版模型。

items.en: The evaluation metric uses a normalized score averaged over 10 episodes per task and method, where an episode receives a score of 1.0 for a full success, and a fractional score for partial success. For example, the score for bussing is the fraction of objects that are correctly placed in the proper receptacle. We describe the scoring rubrics in Appendix A-E. The results, shown in Figure 7, show that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} attains by far the best results across the board on all the zero-shot tasks, with near perfect success rates on shirt folding and the easier bussing tasks, and large improvements over all baselines. The “parity” version of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}, which is trained for only 160k steps, still outperforms all the baselines, and even π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small outperforms OpenVLA and Octo. OpenVLA struggles on these tasks because its autoregressive discretization architecture does not support action chunks. The UR5e-only OpenVLA model performs better, but is still far below the performance of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}. Octo does support action chunks, but has a comparatively limited representational capacity. This comparison illustrates the importance of combining large, expressive architectures with the ability to model complex distributions via flow matching or diffusion. Additionally, the comparison to π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small illustrates the importance of incorporating VLM pre-training. Unfortunately, it is hard to make this last comparison fair: π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small uses fewer parameters, but larger models are difficult to use without pre-training. Overall, these experiments show that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} provides a powerful pre-trained model with the ability to effectively perform a variety of tasks with a variety of robots, with much better performance than prior models.
items.zh: 评估指标使用每个任务和方法10个episode的归一化分数平均值，其中完全成功的episode得分为1.0，部分成功得分为分数。例如，清理任务的分数是正确放入适当容器的物体比例。我们在附录A-E中描述了评分标准。结果如图7所示，显示π0在所有零样本任务上取得了迄今为止最好的结果，在折叠衬衫和较简单的清理任务上接近完美的成功率，并大幅优于所有基线。仅训练160k步的“算力对等”版本π0仍然优于所有基线，甚至π0-small也优于OpenVLA和Octo。OpenVLA在这些任务上表现不佳，因为其自回归离散化架构不支持动作块。仅UR5e的OpenVLA模型表现更好，但仍远低于π0的性能。Octo支持动作块，但表示能力相对有限。这一比较说明了将大型、表达性强的架构与通过流匹配或扩散建模复杂分布的能力相结合的重要性。此外，与π0-small的比较说明了融入VLM预训练的重要性。不幸的是，很难使这最后的比较公平：π0-small使用更少的参数，但更大的模型在没有预训练的情况下难以使用。总体而言，这些实验表明π0提供了一个强大的预训练模型，能够有效地执行各种机器人的各种任务，性能远优于先前的模型。

items.capEn: Figure 8: The tasks in our language evaluation. We evaluate our model on 3 different language-conditioned tasks, each of which requires following a sequence of intermediate language commands. The tasks involve bussing a table (top) to put dishes in a bin and garbage in a trash bin, setting a table (middle) by taking items out of a bin, and packing a shopping bag (bottom).
items.capZh: 图8：语言评估中的任务。我们在3个不同的语言条件任务上评估模型，每个任务需要遵循一系列中间语言指令。这些任务包括清理餐桌（顶部）将餐具放入餐具箱、垃圾放入垃圾桶，布置餐桌（中间）从箱子中取出物品，以及打包购物袋（底部）。

items.en: In the next set of experiments, we fine-tune the base π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} model to follow language commands in a set of evaluation domains. We compare this fine-tuned π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} model with the π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small model described in Section IV, which we found to be the strongest baseline in the previous section. Recall that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small does not use a VLM initialization. This experiment therefore aims to measure how much VLM pre-training boosts our model’s ability to follow language instructions. Note that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small is also a significantly smaller model — unfortunately, it is difficult to remove this confounder, because VLM initialization serves both to make it practical to train a much larger model without overfitting, and to improve language instruction following. We nonetheless hope that this experiment sheds light on the language capabilities of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}. The language instructions for each task consist of objects to pick up and locations to place those objects, with language-labeled segments that are about 2 seconds in length. Each full task consists of numerous such segments. The tasks in this evaluation consist of:
items.zh: 在下一组实验中，我们微调基础π0模型以遵循一组评估领域中的语言指令。我们将这个微调后的π0模型与第IV节描述的π0-small模型进行比较，我们发现后者是上一节中最强的基线。回想一下，π0-small不使用VLM初始化。因此，本实验旨在衡量VLM预训练在多大程度上提升了模型遵循语言指令的能力。注意，π0-small也是一个明显更小的模型——不幸的是，很难消除这个混淆因素，因为VLM初始化既使得训练更大的模型而不过拟合成为可能，也提高了语言指令遵循能力。尽管如此，我们希望这个实验能揭示π0的语言能力。每个任务的语言指令包括要拾取的物体和放置这些物体的位置，语言标记的片段大约持续2秒。每个完整任务由许多这样的片段组成。本评估中的任务包括：

items.capEn: Figure 9: Language evaluation. We compare “flat” versions of our policies, −𝚏𝚕𝚊𝚝𝚏𝚕𝚊𝚝\mathtt{-flat}, which receive only the overall task command (e.g., “bag the groceries”) with a method that receives intermediate commands from a human expert, −𝚑𝚞𝚖𝚊𝚗𝚑𝚞𝚖𝚊𝚗\mathtt{-human}, or a high-level VLM policy, −𝙷𝙻𝙷𝙻\mathtt{-HL}. We also compare our model to a small non-VLM variant under the “expert” condition, π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} and π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small, in terms of language following accuracy. The results show a significant improvement with π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} from intermediate language commands provided by a human expert and to a lesser degree by an autonomous high-level policy. Notably, due to π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small’s limited language following ability, overall it does not gain with the addition of a high-level expert.
items.capZh: 图9：语言评估。我们比较了策略的“扁平”版本，−𝚏𝚕𝚊𝚝，它只接收整体任务指令（例如，“把杂货装袋”），以及接收来自人类专家的中间指令的方法−𝚑𝚞𝚖𝚊𝚗，或高级VLM策略−𝙷𝙻。我们还将我们的模型与一个小的非VLM变体在“专家”条件下进行比较，即π0和π0-small，在语言遵循准确性方面。结果显示，π0在来自人类专家的中间语言指令下显著改进，在较小程度上来自自主高级策略。值得注意的是，由于π0-small的语言遵循能力有限，总体上它并没有因添加高级专家而获益。

items.en: Bussing: the robot must clean a table, placing dishes and cutlery in a bin, and trash into a trash bin.
items.zh: 清理：机器人必须清理桌子，将餐具和刀具放入餐具箱，垃圾放入垃圾桶。

items.en: Grocery bagging: the robot must pack grocery items, such as bags of coffee beans, barley, marshmallow, seaweed, almonds, spaghetti, and cans into a bag.
items.zh: 装袋杂货：机器人必须将杂货物品装袋，例如袋装咖啡豆、大麦、棉花糖、海苔、杏仁、意大利面和罐头。

items.en: In Figure 8, we show the language-conditioned tasks in our evaluation and present the evaluation results. We evaluate five different conditions. π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-flat (and π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small-flat) corresponds to directly command the model with the task description (e.g., “bag the groceries”), without intermediate language commands. π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-human (and π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small-human) provides intermediate step commands (e.g., which object to pick and where to place it) from an expert human user. These conditions evaluate each model’s ability to follow more detailed language commands: while these intermediate commands provide considerable information for how to perform the task, the model must be able to understand and follow those commands to benefit from them. Finally, π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-HL evaluates π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} with high-level commands provided by a high-level VLM, as discussed in Section V-B. This condition is also autonomous, without any human expert.
items.zh: 在图8中，我们展示了评估中的语言条件任务并呈现了评估结果。我们评估了五种不同条件。π0-flat（以及π0-small-flat）对应于直接用任务描述（例如，“将杂货装袋”）命令模型，无需中间语言命令。π0-human（以及π0-small-human）提供来自专家人类用户的中间步骤命令（例如，拾取哪个物体以及放置到哪里）。这些条件评估每个模型遵循更详细语言命令的能力：虽然这些中间命令提供了如何执行任务的大量信息，但模型必须能够理解并遵循这些命令才能从中受益。最后，π0-HL评估了π0使用高级VLM提供的高级命令，如第V-B节所述。该条件也是自主的，无需任何人类专家。

items.en: The results in Figure 9, averaging over 10 trials per task, show that the language following accuracy of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} is significantly better than that of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-small. This suggests a significant improvement from the larger pre-trained VLM initialization. This capability translates to an improvement in performance with expert human guidance (π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-human) and with high-level model guidance (π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}-HL). The results indicate that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}’s language following ability directly translates into better autonomous performance on complex tasks with high-level guidance.
items.zh: 图9的结果（每个任务平均10次试验）表明，π0的语言跟随准确率显著优于π0-small。这表明更大的预训练VLM初始化带来了显著改进。这种能力转化为在专家人类指导（π0-human）和高级模型指导（π0-HL）下性能的提升。结果表明，π0的语言跟随能力直接转化为在高级指导下复杂任务上更好的自主性能。

full.secZh: 六-C 学习新的灵巧任务

items.capEn: Figure 10: Fine-tuning evaluation tasks: We fine-tune our model to a variety of downstream tasks that are distinct from the tasks seen in pre-training. Our tasks represent a range of similarity from the pre-training tasks, with tasks that are most similar to pre-training (stack bowls and towel folding), a task that introduces an unseen new element (a microwave), and tasks that require new motions and new object types (Franka items in drawer and paper towel replacement).
items.capZh: 图10：微调评估任务：我们将模型微调到与预训练任务不同的各种下游任务。我们的任务代表了与预训练任务相似度的范围，包括与预训练最相似的任务（叠碗和毛巾折叠）、引入未见新元素的任务（微波炉），以及需要新动作和新物体类型的任务（Franka物品放入抽屉和纸巾更换）。

items.en: In the next set of experiments, we evaluate our model on new tasks that differ significantly from the pre-training data, requiring entirely new behaviors. For these evaluations, we fine-tune the model using various amounts of data for each new task. While each task is new, we partition the tasks into “tiers” depending on how much they differ from tasks in the pre-training data. The tasks, shown in Figure 10, are:
items.zh: 在下一组实验中，我们评估模型在与预训练数据显著不同的新任务上的表现，这些任务需要全新的行为。对于这些评估，我们使用每个新任务的不同数据量对模型进行微调。虽然每个任务都是新的，但我们根据它们与预训练数据中任务的差异程度将任务划分为“层级”。这些任务如图10所示：

items.capEn: Figure 11: Fine-tuning with varying amounts of data. π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} can learn some easier tasks even with smaller amounts of data, and the pre-trained model often attains a larger improvement over the model trained from scratch.
items.capZh: 图11：使用不同数据量的微调。π0即使使用较少的数据也能学习一些较容易的任务，并且预训练模型通常比从头训练的模型获得更大的改进。

items.en: UR5e stack bowls. This task requires stacking bowls, with four bowls of different sizes. Since this task requires grasping and moving dishes like the bussing task in the pre-training data, we place it in the “easy” tier. The training data contains a variety of bowls, and the evaluations use a mix of seen and unseen bowls.
items.zh: UR5e叠碗。此任务需要叠放四个不同大小的碗。由于此任务需要像预训练数据中的餐具清理任务一样抓取和移动餐具，我们将其归为“容易”层级。训练数据包含各种碗，评估使用已见和未见碗的混合。

items.en: Towel folding. This task requires folding a towel. Since this is similar to shirt folding, which is present in pre-training, we place it in the “easy” tier.
items.zh: 毛巾折叠。此任务需要折叠毛巾。由于这与预训练中存在的衬衫折叠相似，我们将其归为“容易”层级。

items.en: Tupperware in microwave. This task requires opening a microwave, putting a plastic container inside it, and closing it. The containers come in different shapes and colors, and the evaluations use a mix of seen and unseen containers. The container manipulation resembles pre-training data, but the microwave is not found in pre-training.
items.zh: 微波炉中的特百惠。此任务需要打开微波炉，将塑料容器放入其中，然后关闭。容器有不同的形状和颜色，评估使用已见和未见容器的混合。容器操作类似于预训练数据，但微波炉在预训练中未出现。

items.en: Paper towel replacement. This task requires removing an old cardboard paper towel tube from a holder and replacing it with a fresh paper towel roll. Because no such items are found in pre-training, we consider this “hard.”
items.zh: 纸巾更换。此任务需要从支架上取下旧的纸板纸巾管，并更换新的纸巾卷。由于预训练中没有此类物品，我们认为这是“困难”的。

items.en: Franka items in drawer. This task requires opening a drawer, packing items into a drawer, and closing it. Because there is no similar task with the Franka robot in pre-training, we consider this “hard.”
items.zh: Franka物品放入抽屉。此任务需要打开抽屉，将物品装入抽屉，然后关闭。由于预训练中没有与Franka机器人相似的任务，我们认为这是“困难”的。

items.en: We compare our model after fine-tuning both to OpenVLA [24] and Octo [50], which also employ a pre-training and fine-tuning recipe. Since our aim is to evaluate the specific models (rather than the architectures), we use the publicly available pre-trained checkpoints for these models, which are trained on OXE [10], and then fine-tune them to each task. We also compare to ACT [57] and Diffusion Policy [9], which are designed specifically for learning dexterous tasks from smaller datasets. ACT and Diffusion Policy are trained only on the fine-tuning datasets, which are of similar size to the individual datasets used in the ACT and Diffusion Policy experiments [9, 57]. We evaluate π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} by fine-tuning from our pre-trained base model, as well as by training from scratch. This comparison is meant to evaluate the individual benefits of the π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} architecture and our pre-training procedure. We hypothesize that the π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} architecture with VLM initialization should already provide a stronger starting point for the individual tasks, while the pre-training procedure should further improve its performance, especially with smaller fine-tuning datasets.
items.zh: 我们将微调后的模型与OpenVLA [24]和Octo [50]进行比较，它们也采用了预训练和微调方案。由于我们的目标是评估特定模型（而非架构），我们使用这些模型公开可用的预训练检查点（在OXE [10]上训练），然后将它们微调到每个任务。我们还与ACT [57]和Diffusion Policy [9]进行比较，它们专门设计用于从较小数据集学习灵巧任务。ACT和Diffusion Policy仅在微调数据集上训练，其大小与ACT和Diffusion Policy实验中使用的单个数据集相似[9, 57]。我们通过从预训练基础模型微调以及从头训练来评估π0。此比较旨在评估π0架构和预训练过程的各自优势。我们假设，具有VLM初始化的π0架构已经为各个任务提供了更强的起点，而预训练过程应进一步提高其性能，尤其是在微调数据集较小的情况下。

full.secZh: 六-D 掌握复杂多阶段任务

items.capEn: Figure 12: We evaluate a range of complex and temporally extended tasks. This includes: folding laundry from a bin with a stationary (a) or mobile (b) robot, bussing a real lunch table (c), assembling a box (d), packing eggs into a carton (e), and packing food into a to-go box (f). These tasks require combining dozens of individual behaviors, such as grasping, stacking, folding, and flattening, generalization to a huge variety of object configurations, and complex physical properties, such as deformable objects or flexible cardboard.
items.capZh: 图12：我们评估了一系列复杂且时间上扩展的任务。这包括：使用固定式（a）或移动式（b）机器人从篮子中折叠衣物、清理真实午餐桌（c）、组装盒子（d）、将鸡蛋装入纸盒（e）以及将食物装入外卖盒（f）。这些任务需要结合数十种个体行为，如抓取、堆叠、折叠和压平，泛化到各种物体配置，以及复杂的物理特性，如可变形物体或柔性纸板。

items.en: In our final set of experiments, we tackle a range of challenging multi-stage tasks via a combination of fine-tuning and language. For some of these tasks, data is present in pre-training, but fine-tuning is required to attain mastery. For some, no data is present in pre-training. The tasks in this evaluation, shown in Figure 12, are:
items.zh: 在最后一组实验中，我们通过微调和语言的组合处理一系列具有挑战性的多阶段任务。对于其中一些任务，预训练中存在数据，但需要微调才能掌握。对于另一些任务，预训练中没有数据。此评估中的任务如图12所示：

items.capEn: Figure 13: Post-training results on complex tasks in terms of average scores over 10 trials. The full pre-trained π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} model attains more than 50% of the maximum score across all of the tasks, and typically outperforms the ablations, with especially significant improvements on the hardest tasks.
items.capZh: 图13：复杂任务的后训练结果，以10次试验的平均分数表示。完全预训练的π0模型在所有任务上获得了超过50%的最高分数，并且通常优于消融模型，在最困难的任务上尤其显著。

items.en: Laundry folding: This task requires a static (non-mobile) bimanual system to fold articles of clothing. The clothing items start in a randomized crumpled state in a bin, and the goal is to take out the item, fold it, and place it on top of a stack of previously folded items. The randomized initial configuration of the crumpled laundry presents a major challenge, since the policy needs to generalize to any configuration. This task is present in pre-training.
items.zh: 衣物折叠：此任务需要静态（非移动）双臂系统折叠衣物。衣物物品以随机揉皱的状态开始放在篮子中，目标是取出物品，折叠，然后放在先前折叠物品的堆叠上。揉皱衣物的随机初始配置构成了主要挑战，因为策略需要泛化到任何配置。此任务存在于预训练中。

items.en: Mobile laundry: Here, the Fibocom mobile robot in Figure 5 has to fold laundry, facing many of the same challenges while controlling orientation and translation. This task is present in pre-training.
items.zh: 移动衣物折叠：这里，图5中的Fibocom移动机器人需要折叠衣物，面临许多相同的挑战，同时控制方向和移动。此任务存在于预训练中。

items.en: Dryer unloading: Here, the Fibocom mobile robot has to take laundry out of a dryer and place it into a hamper. This task is present in pre-training.
items.zh: 烘干机卸货：这里，Fibocom移动机器人需要从烘干机中取出衣物并放入洗衣篮。此任务存在于预训练中。

items.en: Box building: The robot has to assemble a cardboard box that starts in a flattened state. This task presents a number of major challenges: the box needs to bent in the right way, and the robot needs to hold down parts of the box while folding others, utilizing both arms and even the surface of the table to brace during folding motions. The robot might need to retry some folds, requiring a reactive and intelligent strategy. This task is not present in the pre-training data. This task is not present in pre-training.
items.zh: 盒子组装：机器人需要组装一个从扁平状态开始的纸板箱。此任务提出了许多主要挑战：盒子需要以正确的方式弯曲，机器人需要在折叠其他部分时按住盒子的某些部分，利用双臂甚至桌面表面在折叠动作中支撑。机器人可能需要重试某些折叠，这需要反应性和智能策略。此任务不存在于预训练数据中。

items.en: To-go box: This task requires moving several food items from a plate into a to-go box, requiring packing the items into the box so that they do not stick out, and then closing the box with both arms. This task is not present in the pre-training data. This task is not present in pre-training.
items.zh: 外卖盒：此任务需要将几个食物物品从盘子移到外卖盒中，需要将物品装入盒子使其不突出，然后用双臂关闭盒子。此任务不存在于预训练数据中。

items.en: Packing eggs: The robot needs to take six eggs out of a bowl and pack them into an egg carton, and then close the carton. The eggs need to be grasped in a manner appropriate to their pose inside the bowl, and then placed into open slots in the carton. This presents challenges due to the egg shape, slipperiness, and the need for careful placement. Closing the box requires the use of both arms. This task is not present in the pre-training data. This task is not present in pre-training.
items.zh: 装鸡蛋：机器人需要从碗中取出六个鸡蛋并装入鸡蛋盒，然后关闭盒子。鸡蛋需要根据其在碗中的姿态以适当方式抓取，然后放入盒子的开放槽中。由于鸡蛋的形状、光滑度以及需要小心放置，这带来了挑战。关闭盒子需要使用双臂。此任务不存在于预训练数据中。

items.en: The results, showing average scores per task over 10 trials, are presented in Figure 13. The scoring rubrics are in Appendix A-E. A score of 1.0 represents a perfect execution, while partial scores correspond to partially completed tasks (e.g., 0.5 indicates that half the objects were bussed correctly). These tasks are very difficult, and we were not able to solve them with other methods. We therefore use these tasks to compare to ablations of our approach, evaluating π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} after pre-training and fine-tuning, zero-shot evaluation after pre-training only (“zero-shot”), and training on the fine-tuning data without any pre-training (“scratch”). The results show that π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} can solve many of these tasks, with our full pre-training and fine-tuning recipe performing best across the board. Note that many of these more difficult tasks show a very large improvement from using the pre-trained model, indicating that pre-training is especially useful with harder tasks. The absolute performance of π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0} varies across the tasks, likely due to differences in task difficulty and the degree to which the tasks are represented in pre-training. We recommend that readers watch the task videos on the accompanying website for a more complete impression of these tasks and their complexity. We believe that this level of autonomous performance on such challenging tasks represents a new state of the art in dexterous robot manipulation with learned policies.
items.zh: 结果（每项任务在10次试验中的平均得分）如图13所示。评分标准见附录A-E。得分为1.0表示完美执行，部分得分对应部分完成任务（例如，0.5表示一半物体被正确搬运）。这些任务非常困难，我们无法用其他方法解决。因此，我们使用这些任务来比较我们方法的消融实验，评估预训练和微调后的π₀、仅预训练后的零样本评估（“零样本”），以及在没有预训练的情况下仅在微调数据上训练（“从头训练”）。结果表明，π₀能够解决其中许多任务，而我们的完整预训练和微调方案在所有任务中表现最佳。请注意，许多较困难的任务显示，使用预训练模型带来了非常大的改进，表明预训练在更困难的任务中尤其有用。π₀的绝对性能因任务而异，这可能是由于任务难度差异以及任务在预训练中的表示程度不同。我们建议读者观看附带网站上的任务视频，以更全面地了解这些任务及其复杂性。我们相信，在如此具有挑战性的任务上达到这种自主性能水平，代表了基于学习策略的灵巧机器人操作的最新水平。

full.sec: VII Discussion, Limitations, and Future Work
full.secZh: 七、讨论、局限与未来工作

items.en: We presented a framework for training a robot foundation model, which we refer to as π0subscript𝜋0\pi_{0}, that consists of pre-training on highly diverse data, followed by either zero-shot evaluation or fine-tuning to complex downstream tasks. Our empirical evaluation studies tasks that combine dexterity, generalization, and temporally extended multi-stage behaviors. Our model incorporates Internet-scale vision-language model (VLM) pre-training with flow matching for representing complex high-frequency action chunks. Our pre-training mixture consists of 10,000 hours of dexterous manipulation data from 7 different robot configurations and 68 tasks, in addition to large amounts of previously collected robot manipulation data from OXE [10], DROID [23], and Bridge [52]. To our knowledge, this represents the largest pre-training mixture ever used for a robot manipulation model. Our fine-tuning experiments include over 20 tasks, where we show that our model outperforms a variety of baselines, including prior VLA models [24] and models designed specifically for dexterous manipulation [57, 9]. We also examine how our post-training recipe can enable highly complex tasks, such as folding multiple articles of clothing from arbitrary initial configurations or assembling boxes.
items.zh: 我们提出了一个训练机器人基础模型的框架，称为π₀，该框架包括在高度多样化的数据上进行预训练，然后进行零样本评估或微调以适应复杂的下游任务。我们的实证评估研究了结合灵巧性、泛化能力和时间上扩展的多阶段行为的任务。我们的模型将互联网规模的视觉语言模型（VLM）预训练与流匹配相结合，以表示复杂的高频动作块。我们的预训练混合数据集包含来自7种不同机器人配置和68个任务的10,000小时灵巧操作数据，以及来自OXE [10]、DROID [23]和Bridge [52]的大量先前收集的机器人操作数据。据我们所知，这是机器人操作模型有史以来使用的最大的预训练混合数据集。我们的微调实验包括20多个任务，结果表明我们的模型优于多种基线，包括先前的VLA模型[24]以及专门为灵巧操作设计的模型[57, 9]。我们还研究了我们的后训练方案如何能够实现高度复杂的任务，例如从任意初始配置折叠多件衣物或组装盒子。

items.en: Our framework broadly resembles the training procedures employed for large language models, which typically consist of pre-training a base model on very large datasets scraped from the web, followed by a post-training procedure that aims to “align” the model to enable it to follow instructions and perform user commands. It is generally recognized that most of the “knowledge” in such models is acquired in the pre-training phase, while the post-training phase serves to tell the model how it should leverage that knowledge to fulfill user commands. Our experiments imply that an analogous phenomenon might take place with robot foundation models, where pre-trained models have some zero-shot capabilities, but complex tasks like laundry following require fine-tuning with high-quality data. Training on only this high-quality data results in a brittle model that does not reliably recover from mistakes, while running the pre-trained model in zero shot does not always exhibit the fluent strategies demonstrated in the post-training data.
items.zh: 我们的框架大致类似于大型语言模型采用的训练流程，通常包括在从网络抓取的大规模数据集上预训练基础模型，然后进行后训练过程，旨在“对齐”模型，使其能够遵循指令并执行用户命令。普遍认为，此类模型中的大部分“知识”是在预训练阶段获得的，而后训练阶段则用于告诉模型如何利用这些知识来满足用户命令。我们的实验表明，机器人基础模型可能发生类似现象：预训练模型具有一定的零样本能力，但像叠衣服这样的复杂任务需要高质量数据的微调。仅在此高质量数据上训练会导致模型脆弱，无法可靠地从错误中恢复，而零样本运行预训练模型并不总是能展现出后训练数据中的流畅策略。

items.en: We hope that our results will serve as a stepping stone toward general and broadly applicable robot foundation models. Our experiments suggest that such models may soon be a reality, but there are a number of limitations and ample room for future work. First, our experiments do not yet provide a comprehensive understanding of how the pre-training datasets should be composed: we combined all data available to us, but understanding what type of data is more helpful to add and how it should be weighted remains an open problem. Not all tasks in our evaluation work reliably, and it remains unclear how to predict how much and what kind of data is needed to attain near-perfect performance. Finally, it remains to be seen how much positive transfer there is in combining highly diverse data, particularly from different tasks and different robots: although our results suggest that universal pre-trained robot foundation models might become a reality, it is left for future work to understand whether this universality extends to much more distinct domains, such as autonomous driving, navigation, and legged locomotion.
items.zh: 我们希望我们的结果能成为迈向通用且广泛适用的机器人基础模型的垫脚石。我们的实验表明，此类模型可能很快成为现实，但仍存在许多局限性和未来工作的空间。首先，我们的实验尚未全面理解预训练数据集应如何组成：我们合并了所有可用数据，但了解哪种类型的数据更有助于添加以及应如何加权仍然是一个开放问题。我们评估中的并非所有任务都能可靠工作，并且仍不清楚如何预测需要多少和何种数据才能达到近乎完美的性能。最后，结合高度多样化的数据（特别是来自不同任务和不同机器人的数据）能产生多少正迁移仍有待观察：尽管我们的结果表明通用预训练机器人基础模型可能成为现实，但这种通用性是否扩展到更不同的领域（如自动驾驶、导航和腿式运动）仍有待未来研究。
